mnemonics.title: Bubble Sort Visualization
mnemonics.content: BUBBLES RISE UP - LARGER ELEMENTS BUBBLE UP TO THE END IN EACH PASS

mnemonics.title: Selection Sort Steps
mnemonics.content: SELECT THE MINIMUM - FIND IT, SWAP IT, MOVE ON

mnemonics.title: Quick Sort Pattern
mnemonics.content: QUICK: QUICKLY PICK A PIVOT, PARTITION AROUND IT, RECURSE ON BOTH SIDES

mnemonics.title: Merge Sort Pattern
mnemonics.content: MERGE: MERELY DIVIDE IN HALF, SORT EACH, MERGE TOGETHER

mnemonics.title: Stable vs Unstable
mnemonics.content: STABLE SAILS THROUGH: Selection, Timsort, Quicksort are Unstable; Merge, Insertion, Bubble, Counting are Stable

acronyms.expansion: Think, Implement, Measure, Examine - Compare, Optimize, Exchange, Maximize Efficiency
acronyms.usage: Remembering sorting algorithm characteristics and trade-offs

acronyms.expansion: Stable Types: Merge, Insert, Bubble, Counting, Radix
acronyms.usage: Recalling which sorting algorithms maintain relative order of equal elements

acronyms.expansion: In-Place Needs: Only O(1) Extra - Bubble, Selection, Insertion, Shell, Quick, Heap
acronyms.usage: Identifying which algorithms use constant extra space

visualTips.visualization: IMAGINE AIR BUBBLES RISING IN WATER - THE LARGEST ELEMENTS FLOAT TO THE TOP (END) WITH EACH PASS. AFTER FIRST PASS, LARGEST IS AT END; AFTER SECOND, SECOND LARGEST IS AT POSITION n-1, ETC.
IMAGINE SELECTING THE BEST PLAYER FROM A LINE - FIND THE MINIMUM, PUT AT FRONT, REPEAT WITH REMAINING. LIKE PICKING CARDS FROM A DECK IN ORDER.
IMAGINE SORTING CARDS IN YOUR HAND - YOU PICK ONE CARD AND INSERT IT IN ITS CORRECT POSITION AMONG ALREADY SORTED CARDS.
IMAGINE DIVIDING A PIZZA INTO SMALL SLICES, SORTING EACH SLICE, THEN COMBINING (MERGING) THEM BACK IN ORDER - LIKE A RECURSIVE FOLDING AND UNFOLDING.
IMAGINE PARTITIONING BOOKS ON A SHELF BY HEIGHT - CHOOSE A PIVOT (BOOK), PLACE ALL SHORTER BOOKS TO LEFT, TALLER TO RIGHT, REPEAT FOR EACH SIDE.
IMAGINE A PRIORITY QUEUE - BUILD A MAX-HEAP, REMOVE MAXIMUM REPEATEDLY, AND THE ARRAY BECOMES SORTED FROM END TO BEGINNING.

analogies.analogy: SORTING ALGORITHMS ARE LIKE ORGANIZING A LIBRARY: BUBBLE SORT IS LIKE COMPARING ADJACENT BOOKS AND SWAPPING; SELECTION SORT IS LIKE FINDING THE SHORTEST BOOK AND PLACING IT FIRST; INSERTION SORT IS LIKE SORTING HANDFULS OF CARDS; QUICK SORT IS LIKE PUTTING BOOKS AROUND A PIVOT BOOK BY HEIGHT.
DIVIDE AND CONQUER IN SORTING IS LIKE THE PHILOSOPHY OF 'BREAK IT DOWN TO SOLVE IT' - JUST AS YOU WOULD ORGANIZE A LARGE CROWD BY FIRST DIVIDING INTO SMALL GROUPS, SORTING EACH GROUP, THEN COMBINING.

STABLE SORTING IS LIKE MAINTAINING ALPHABETICAL ORDER AMONG STUDENTS WITH SAME LAST NAME - IF TWO STUDENTS NAMED 'SMITH' WERE IN ORDER (JOHN, MARY) BEFORE SORTING, THEY STAY IN THAT ORDER AFTER SORTING BY GRADE.
IN-PLACE SORTING IS LIKE ORGANIZING A DESK WITH ONLY THE ITEMS ON THE DESK; EXTERNAL SORTING IS LIKE NEEDING AN EXTRA ROOM TO SORT FILES BECAUSE THEY DON'T ALL FIT ON THE DESK.
